title: Introduction to Electrical Engineering & Power Utilisation

prerequisites.text: PHY1013F/S (MEC), MAM1021S (MEC), PHY1004W or PHY1032S (CSC), MAM1004F (>60%) and MAM1008S (>60%) or MAM1000W (CSC).

outline: This course aims to develop an understanding of: (a) DC circuits and networks including series and parallel circuits, Kirchhoff's laws, Mesh Analysis, Superposition Theorem, Star-to-delta and Delta-to-star Transformation, Thevenin's theorem, Maximum Power Transfer theorem, DC transients in R-L and R-C circuits; (b) Fundamentals of AC including generation, waveform concepts, period, frequency, angular velocity, phase, average, peak and RMS values; (c) Single-phase AC circuits including AC through resistance, inductance and capacitance, reactance and impedance, phasors, active, reactive and apparent power, and power factor; (d) Three-phase AC systems; (e) Magnetic circuits; (f) Single-phase transformers including construction, principle of operation, EMF equation, transformation ratio, equivalent circuits, voltage regulation, losses and efficiency; (g) DC motors including principle of operation, types, speed and torque characteristics and speed control.
